vulnerabilities.code_context:
Pointer 'buf' is first freed at source line 8.
Pointer 'buf' is freed again at source line 11: free(buf);  // CWE-415: double free
vulnerabilities.trigger_cond: Execution reaches free(buf) twice without reset or reallocation.
vulnerabilities.fix_advice: Set buf to NULL after free and avoid duplicate ownership.

vulnerabilities.code_context:
Function 'printf' uses a non-literal format argument at source line 7: printf(user_input);  // CWE-134: attacker-controlled format string
Format argument expression: user_input
vulnerabilities.trigger_cond: Attacker-controlled input may be interpreted as a format string, enabling memory disclosure or arbitrary writes via %x/%s/%n tokens.
vulnerabilities.fix_advice: Use a constant format string such as printf("%s", input), fprintf(stream, "%s", input), or snprintf(buf, size, "%s", input).

vulnerabilities.code_context:
Dangerous copy-style function call at source line 9: strcpy(buf, input);  // CWE-122: heap buffer overflow
Rule fallback cannot fully prove destination bounds.
vulnerabilities.trigger_cond: Oversized or attacker-controlled input may reach a copy operation without sufficient length validation.
vulnerabilities.fix_advice: Validate length before copying and use bounded APIs with explicit destination size.

vulnerabilities.code_context:

Rule fallback cannot fully prove destination bounds.
vulnerabilities.trigger_cond: Oversized or attacker-controlled input may reach a copy operation without sufficient length validation.
vulnerabilities.fix_advice: Validate length before copying and use bounded APIs with explicit destination size.

vulnerabilities.code_context:
Pointer 'p' is freed at source line 10.
Pointer 'p' is used again at source line 13: printf("%d\n", *p);  // CWE-416: use after free
vulnerabilities.trigger_cond: Pointer 'p' is dereferenced or accessed after free.
vulnerabilities.fix_advice: Do not access p after free; set it to NULL and guard future uses.
